# chrony-rs-core

The deterministic time-discipline **brain** of [chrony-rs](../../README.md): a
forensic reconstruction of chrony 4.5's internals that runs *without* a host
clock, sockets, or privileges (the one documented exception is synchronous DNS in
`nameserv`). Everything that touches the host is injected behind a trait or closure
so the logic is testable against the real C and against protocol/spec oracles.

This file is **generated** (`cargo xtask gen`) and freshness-gated ‚Äî the lists
below come from the port-parity matrix, so they cannot disagree with what is
actually ported. See the [full matrix](../../docs/generated/port-parity.md) and the
[per-function gap view](../../docs/generated/port-parity-functions.md).

## Fully ported chrony translation units (59)

Every function in these units has a court-backed counterpart (differential tests
against the real compiled C and/or protocol-spec vectors):

- `cmdparse.c` ‚Üí `config/parser.rs`, `cmdparse.rs` ‚Äî command/config line parsing (CPS_*)
- `ntp_io.c` ‚Üí `ntp/packet.rs`, `ptp.rs` ‚Äî NTP socket send/recv path
- `pktlength.c` ‚Üí `pktlength.rs` ‚Äî cmdmon request/reply length tables (PKL_*)
- `ntp_io_linux.c` ‚Üí `ntp_io_linux.rs` ‚Äî Linux HW/kernel RX timestamping
- `ntp_ext.c` ‚Üí `ntp/ext.rs` ‚Äî NTP extension-field (RFC 7822) framing (NEF_*)
- `ntp_auth.c` ‚Üí `ntp_auth.rs` ‚Äî NTP authentication (MAC/NTS dispatch) (NAU_*)
- `ntp_signd.c` ‚Üí `ntp_signd.rs` ‚Äî Samba MS-SNTP signing-daemon bridge (NSD_*)
- `ntp_sources.c` ‚Üí `ntp_sources.rs` ‚Äî NTP source record add/remove/pool (NSR_*)
- `sources.c` ‚Üí `sources/registry.rs`, `sources/combine.rs`, `sources/source.rs`, `sources/reachability.rs`, `sources/selection.rs` ‚Äî source reachability + selection (SRC_*)
- `sourcestats.c` ‚Üí `sourcestats.rs` ‚Äî per-source regression statistics (SST_*)
- `regress.c` ‚Üí `regress.rs` ‚Äî robust linear regression + statistical primitives
- `samplefilt.c` ‚Üí `samplefilt.rs` ‚Äî per-source NTP sample filtering (SPF_*)
- `quantiles.c` ‚Üí `quantiles.rs` ‚Äî streaming (stochastic) quantile estimator
- `reference.c` ‚Üí `reference.rs`, `report.rs`, `clock.rs` ‚Äî tracking + drift state, leap handling (REF_*)
- `local.c` ‚Üí `local.rs` ‚Äî local clock hub: read/cook time, discipline, handlers (LCL_*)
- `smooth.c` ‚Üí `smooth.rs` ‚Äî served-time smoothing (SMT_*)
- `tempcomp.c` ‚Üí `tempcomp.rs` ‚Äî temperature compensation (TMC_*)
- `sched.c` ‚Üí `sched.rs` ‚Äî timer/event scheduler (SCH_*)
- `cmdmon.c` ‚Üí `cmdmon.rs` ‚Äî control/monitoring protocol server (candm)
- `privops.c` ‚Üí `privops.rs`, `chrony-rs-io/privops.rs` ‚Äî privilege-separation helper (PRV_*)
- `array.c` ‚Üí `array.rs` ‚Äî generic dynamic array (ARR_*)
- `memory.c` ‚Üí `memory.rs` ‚Äî xmalloc/xrealloc wrappers
- `stubs.c` ‚Üí ‚Äî ‚Äî test-harness stub implementations
- `keys.c` ‚Üí `keys.rs` ‚Äî symmetric key store (KEY_*)
- `md5.c` ‚Üí `md5.rs` ‚Äî MD5 digest (RFC 1321 reference, NTP symmetric-key auth)
- `hash_intmd5.c` ‚Üí `hash_intmd5.rs` ‚Äî internal MD5 hash backend (HSH_*)
- `hash_gnutls.c` ‚Üí `hash_gnutls.rs` ‚Äî gnutls hash backend
- `hash_nettle.c` ‚Üí `hash_nettle.rs` ‚Äî nettle hash backend
- `hash_nss.c` ‚Üí `hash_nss.rs` ‚Äî NSS hash backend
- `hash_tomcrypt.c` ‚Üí `hash_tomcrypt.rs` ‚Äî tomcrypt hash backend
- `cmac_gnutls.c` ‚Üí `cmac_gnutls.rs` ‚Äî gnutls CMAC backend
- `cmac_nettle.c` ‚Üí `cmac_nettle.rs` ‚Äî AES-CMAC keyed-MAC instance API (CMC_*)
- `nts_ke_client.c` ‚Üí `nts_ke_record.rs` ‚Äî NTS-KE client message logic
- `nts_ke_server.c` ‚Üí `nts_ke_record.rs`, `nts_ke_cookie.rs`, `nts_ke_keydump.rs` ‚Äî NTS-KE server message logic + cookie codec + key dump
- `nts_ke_session.c` ‚Üí `nts_ke_record.rs` ‚Äî NTS-KE TLS session + record codec
- `nts_ntp_auth.c` ‚Üí `nts_ntp_auth.rs` ‚Äî NTS authenticator + encrypted-EEF extension field (NNA_*)
- `nts_ntp_client.c` ‚Üí `nts_ntp_client.rs` ‚Äî client-side NTS-NTP authentication (NNC_*)
- `nts_ntp_server.c` ‚Üí `nts_ntp_server.rs` ‚Äî server-side NTS-NTP authentication (NNS_*)
- `siv_gnutls.c` ‚Üí `siv_gnutls.rs` ‚Äî SIV-AEAD (gnutls)
- `siv_nettle.c` ‚Üí `siv_nettle.rs` ‚Äî SIV AEAD instance API (SIV_*)
- `siv_nettle_int.c` ‚Üí `siv_nettle_int.rs` ‚Äî AES-SIV-CMAC-256 AEAD (RFC 5297)
- `refclock.c` ‚Üí `refclock.rs` ‚Äî reference-clock framework (RCL_*)
- `refclock_shm.c` ‚Üí `refclock_shm.rs` ‚Äî SHM refclock driver (ntpd/gpsd shared-memory protocol)
- `refclock_sock.c` ‚Üí `refclock_sock.rs` ‚Äî SOCK refclock driver (gpsd Unix-datagram sample protocol)
- `rtc.c` ‚Üí `rtc.rs` ‚Äî RTC abstraction layer (RTC_*)
- `rtc_linux.c` ‚Üí `rtc_linux.rs` ‚Äî Linux RTC driver (drift regression)
- `hwclock.c` ‚Üí `hwclock.rs` ‚Äî hardware-clock tracking (HCL_*)
- `sys.c` ‚Üí `sys.rs` ‚Äî OS adapter dispatch
- `sys_generic.c` ‚Üí `sys_generic.rs` ‚Äî generic software-slew clock-discipline driver
- `sys_linux.c` ‚Üí `sys_linux.rs` ‚Äî Linux clock adapter (adjtimex)
- `sys_timex.c` ‚Üí `sys_timex.rs` ‚Äî adjtimex()/ntp_adjtime() clock driver
- `sys_null.c` ‚Üí `sys_null.rs` ‚Äî null clock driver (the `-x` 'disabled control' driver)
- `sys_netbsd.c` ‚Üí `sys_netbsd.rs` ‚Äî NetBSD clock adapter
- `sys_solaris.c` ‚Üí `sys_solaris.rs` ‚Äî Solaris clock adapter
- `socket.c` ‚Üí `socket.rs`, `chrony-rs-io/socket.rs` ‚Äî socket abstraction layer
- `addrfilt.c` ‚Üí `addrfilt.rs` ‚Äî NTP/cmd access-control subnet trie (ADF_*)
- `nameserv.c` ‚Üí `nameserv.rs` ‚Äî synchronous DNS resolution
- `clientlog.c` ‚Üí `clientlog.rs` ‚Äî client access log / rate limiting
- `manual.c` ‚Üí `manual.rs` ‚Äî manual time input / settime (MNL_*)

## Partially ported (6)

Behavior ported with at least one executable court, but not every function (see the
matrix for the exact gap):

- `conf.c` ‚Üí `config/parser.rs`, `config/lexer.rs`, `config/diagnostics.rs`, `config/model.rs`, `config/accessors.rs`, `config/mod.rs` ‚Äî config file parser + 93-directive dispatch (CNF_*)
- `ntp_core.c` ‚Üí `ntp/measurements.rs`, `ntp/packet.rs`, `ntp/poll.rs`, `ntp/parse.rs` ‚Äî NTP protocol engine: poll, process-response, offset/delay (NCR_*)
- `client.c` ‚Üí `report.rs`, `client.rs`, `../chronyc-rs/src/main.rs` ‚Äî chronyc CLI: command dispatch + report formatters
- `main.c` ‚Üí `cmdline.rs`, `../chronyd-rs/src/main.rs` ‚Äî daemon entry, arg parsing, lifecycle
- `util.c` ‚Üí `util.rs`, `ntp/timestamp.rs`, `ntp/measurements.rs` ‚Äî time/UTI/byte utilities (UTI_*)
- `logging.c` ‚Üí `logging.rs`, `chrony-rs-io/logging.rs` ‚Äî logging subsystem (LOG_*)

## Invariants

- **`unsafe` blocks:** 127 (scanned across `crates/*/src`).
- **Target chrony oracle:** 4.5.
- **Trace schema:** `chrony-rs-trace-v1`.

Host mutation (clock, sockets, privileges) lives in the binary crates behind narrow
boundaries; see [`chronyd-rs`](../chronyd-rs/README.md) and
[`chronyc-rs`](../chronyc-rs/README.md).


## crates.io

Published at [crates.io/crates/chrony-rs-core](https://crates.io/crates/chrony-rs-core).
Part of the [chrony-rs](https://crates.io/crates/chrony-rs) workspace.
